'''Marcus St Hilaire''' (born 26 January 1977), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Junior"''', is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|fb|wg}} and {{rlp|ce}} in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and at club level for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] (two spells), [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Bradford Bulls]] and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
St Hilaire was born in [[Huddersfield]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, he is of Irish and Saint Lucian descent.

==Huddersfield==
Huddersfield refused to select St Hilaire after he decided to pursue a transfer to Leeds.<ref>{{cite web|title=Huddersfield refuse to select Marcus St Hilaire |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/huddersfield-refuse-to-select-marcus-st-hilaire-1348967.html|website=The Independent|accessdate=19 November 2019}}</ref>

==Leeds==
St Hilaire was transferred from [[Huddersfield Giants]] to [[Leeds Rhinos]] in July 1996. "Junior", as he was known, became an integral part of the Leeds squad, especially under coach [[Graham Murray]]. He was voted the Yorkshire Evening Post / Leeds Rhinos Shooting Star of 1998 as the most improved player.<ref name="leedsprofile" /> and played for [[Leeds Rhinos]] from the substitutes' bench in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors]].

A brilliant runner with the ball, all too often injuries prevented him from gaining the confidence he needed to shine for the Leeds Rhinos after 1999, the high point of his time at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]]. In total he made 65 starts for Leeds Rhinos plus 46 substitute appearances leading to a total of 111 appearances. He scored 39 tries for a total of 156 points. St Hilaire was a try scorer at Wembley in the 1999 [[Challenge Cup]] Final when he was used in his normal role of coming on at full back to allow [[Iestyn Harris]] to move up to stand off in the second half. He played 2-matches for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the 1999 [[Anglo-French Challenge]]. Although never having played rugby union at the top level, St Hilaire was selected for the [[England national rugby sevens team|England Sevens team]] in the [[2002 Commonwealth Games]], although he did not play in any of their four games.<ref name="St Hilaire books sevens place">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport3/commonwealthgames2002/hi/rugby_7s/newsid_2082000/2082326.stm|title=St Hilaire books sevens place|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=31 December 2012|access-date=1 January 2013}}</ref>

==Huddersfield==
Midway through the 2002's [[Super League VII]], St Hilaire was released from the final year of his contract at the Leeds Rhinos and re-joined the Huddersfield Giants,<ref>{{cite news |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=St Hilaire takes on tall order with ambitious Giants |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2002/aug/24/rugbyleague.huddersfieldgiants |website=The Guardian|date=24 August 2002 |accessdate=19 November 2019}}</ref> where he helped the Huddersfield Giants regain their place in the [[Super League]].

==Later years==
After being released by the Huddersfield Giants after 2005's [[Super League X]], St Hilaire joined [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]. But after playing a small part in a friendly game for them, he signed for the [[Bradford Bulls]] for 2006's [[Super League XI]] as {{rlp|fb}} cover. St Hilaire left the Bradford Bulls at the end of 2007's [[Super League XII]], joining [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Marcus St Hilaire signs new Oldham Roughyeds deal |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/9194099.stm |website=BBC Sport |date=16 November 2010 |publisher=BBC |access-date=19 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Jackson |first1=Phil |title=St Hilaire joins Oldham |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/2866484/st-hilaire-joins-oldham |website=Sky Sports |publisher=Sky |accessdate=19 November 2019}}</ref> In 2009 he represented [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]]. St Hilaire spent the remainder of his playing career at Oldham, announcing his retirement in 2011.<ref>{{cite web|title=Marcus calls time on 17 years of Rugby|url=http://www.roughyeds.co.uk/latest-news/marcus-calls-time-on-17-years-of-rugby|publisher=Oldham Rugby League Football Club|accessdate=26 January 2014}}</ref>
